info.title: zapo-rest API
info.description:
# zapo-rest

REST API multi-session para WhatsApp baseada em [zapo-js](https://zapo.to).

## Autenticação

| Tipo | Origem | Escopo |
|------|--------|--------|
| **Admin** | env `ADMIN_API_KEY` | Todas as instâncias + create/delete/rotate |
| **Instance** | campo `apiKey` da instância (plaintext) | Somente a instância dona da key |

Envie em **todas** as rotas `/v1/*`:

```http
X-Api-Key: <sua-chave>
```

ou

```http
Authorization: Bearer <sua-chave>
```

`GET /health` e `GET /ready` são públicos.

## Escopo da instância (dual path)

| Quem | Path |
|------|------|
| **Admin** | **Sempre** com nome: `/v1/instances/:name/...` (omitir o nome → 400) |
| **Instance key** | Nomeado **ou** forma curta sem nome |

Forma curta (só com instance key — a instância é inferida da API key):

- Recursos: `/v1/messages/text`, `/v1/chats`, `/v1/contacts`, … (equivale a `/v1/instances/:name/...`)
- Ciclo de vida: `/v1/instance`, `/v1/instance/connect`, `/v1/instance/qr`, … (singular `instance`, não colide com `/v1/instances`)

## Fluxo rápido (instância)

```bash
# 1) Criar (admin)
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"sales-1","webhookUrl":"https://example.com/webhooks/zapo","webhookEvents":["instance.qr","instance.connection","message.inbound","call.incoming"]}'

# 2) Conectar
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/connect" -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"

# 3) QR
curl -s "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/qr" -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"

# 4) Enviar texto (admin: path com nome)
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/messages/text" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"to":"5511999999999","text":"Olá! Mensagem enviada via zapo-rest 👋","linkPreview":true}'

# 4b) Enviar texto (instance key: forma curta sem nome)
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/messages/text" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $INSTANCE_API_KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"to":"5511999999999","text":"Olá! Mensagem enviada via zapo-rest 👋","linkPreview":true}'
```

## Webhooks

Payload envelope:

```json
{
  "event": "message.inbound",
  "instance": "sales-1",
  "timestamp": "2026-07-11T12:10:00.000Z",
  "data": {
    "key": {
      "remoteJid": "5511888888888@s.whatsapp.net",
      "fromMe": false,
      "id": "ABC123"
    },
    "message": {
      "conversation": "Oi"
    },
    "pushName": "Cliente"
  }
}
```

## VoIP (áudio live)

Após `POST.../calls`, abra o WebSocket:

```
ws(s)://<host>/v1/instances/{name}/calls/{callId}/stream?apiKey=<key>
```

- Server → JSON `{ "op": "ready", "sampleRate": 16000, "format": "f32le" }`
- Client ↔ server: frames **binários** Float32 LE mono @ 16 kHz
- Backpressure: JSON `{ "op": "backpressure", "pause": true | false }`

## Erros

Todas as falhas usam:

```json
{ "error": { "code": "UNAUTHORIZED", "message": "…", "details": {} } }
```

Códigos comuns: `UNAUTHORIZED`, `FORBIDDEN`, `NOT_FOUND`, `CONFLICT`, `VALIDATION_ERROR`, `SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE`, `BAD_REQUEST`.

## Mídia (URL · base64 · upload)

Rotas de envio de mídia e avatar aceitam **uma** das fontes:

| Content-Type | Campos |
|--------------|--------|
| `application/json` | `mediaUrl` e/ou `mediaBase64` (+ metadados) |
| `multipart/form-data` | arquivo `file` (aliases: `media`, `audio`, `image`, `document`, `video`, `sticker`) + campos de texto |

Limite: env `MEDIA_UPLOAD_MAX_BYTES` (padrão **100 MiB**).

```bash
# Avatar por upload
curl -s -X PUT "$BASE/v1/profile/image" -H "X-Api-Key: $INSTANCE_API_KEY" \
  -F file=@./avatar.jpg

# Imagem de mensagem por multipart
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/messages/image" -H "X-Api-Key: $INSTANCE_API_KEY" \
  -F to=5511999999999 -F file=@./foto.jpg -F caption="oi"
```

Endpoints: `PUT /v1/profile/image|picture`, `PUT /v1/groups/:id/picture`,
`POST /v1/messages/{image,video,audio,document,sticker}`, `POST /v1/status/send`,
`POST /v1/calls/blast` (WAV).

## OpenAPI

- **UI (Scalar):** [`/docs`](/docs) — escolha *multipart/form-data* no request body para o seletor de arquivo
- **JSON:** [`/docs/json`](/docs/json) — gerado a partir dos schemas Zod + multipart injetado

## Guia rico

Documentação narrativa (arquitetura, fluxos, exemplos): **[/guide](/guide/)**.

apiKey.name: X-Api-Key
apiKey.description: Admin (`ADMIN_API_KEY`) or instance `apiKey`. Preferred authentication method.

bearerAuth.description: Same key as X-Api-Key, sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

get.description:
Returns `200 { "status": "ok" }` when the process is up. **No authentication.**

Use for load balancers / Kubernetes liveness probes.

Checks Postgres connectivity with `SELECT 1`.

- `200 { "status": "ready" }` when the database is reachable
- `503 { "status": "not_ready" }` otherwise

**No authentication.** Use for readiness gates before sending traffic.

Identifies whether the provided API key is **admin** or an **instance** key.

Used by the dashboard after login.

**Responses:**
- Admin: `{ "role": "admin" }`
- Instance: `{ "role": "instance", "instance": { ...full instance including apiKey } }`

**Example**
```bash
curl -s "$BASE/v1/me" -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY"
```

apiKey.description: Instance API key (plaintext). **Always present** on create, rotate, list, and get — never null/omitted. Scope: this instance only. Header `X-Api-Key` or `Authorization: Bearer`.

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL receiving JSON events, or null

webhookEvents.description: Event allow-list. Empty = all. Examples: `instance.qr`, `instance.connection`, `instance.paired`, `instance.logged_out`, `message.inbound`, `call.incoming`, `call.state`, `call.ended`. `*` = all.

status.description: WhatsApp session lifecycle: `created` registered but not connected; `connecting` socket/handshake in progress; `qr` waiting for QR scan; `pairing` pairing-code flow; `open` ready to send/receive; `close` disconnected (credentials kept); `logged_out` device unlinked — re-pair required.

pushName.description: WhatsApp display / push name when known (from session credentials or profile update)

avatarUrl.description: Own avatar URL when known (public storage or authenticated `.../contacts/{phone}/profile-picture` path). Prefers stored full-res (`image`) over `preview`.

pairPhone.description: Optional phone hint for pairing-code flows

lastQr.description: Raw QR payload from last `auth_qr` event — render as QR image on clients

lastQrAt.description: ISO-8601 time when lastQr was updated

callRecordingEnabled.description: Whether call PCM recording is enabled for this instance

instance.description: WhatsApp multi-session instance. Includes `apiKey` (token), `pushName`, and `avatarUrl` when available.

code.description: Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)

post.description:
**Admin only.** Provisions a new WhatsApp session.

- Generates a unique **instance API key** (`apiKey`) — use it for all session operations
- Does **not** open the socket yet — call `POST /v1/instance/connect` with the instance key
- `name` becomes the zapo `sessionId` (stable across restarts)

**Example**
```bash
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"sales-1","webhookUrl":"https://example.com/webhooks/zapo","webhookEvents":["instance.qr","instance.connection","message.inbound","call.incoming"]}'
```

webhookEvents.description: Optional event filter; omit or empty for all events

pairPhone.description: Optional phone digits for later pairing-code use

post.description:
Sends a plain-text WhatsApp message via `client.message.send`.

**Requirements:** instance connected (`status: open`).

**Example body**
```json
{
  "to": "5511999999999",
  "text": "Olá! Mensagem enviada via zapo-rest 👋",
  "linkPreview": true
}
```

to.description: Recipient: digits (`5511999999999`), PN JID (`…@s.whatsapp.net`), group (`…@g.us`), or LID (`…@lid`)

linkPreview.description: Force/disable link preview for URLs in text

mentions.description: JIDs to mention in groups; include `@number` tokens in text

get.summary: Get profile picture (durable storage + TTL revalidation)
get.description:
Returns contact avatar with **bytes in object storage** (deterministic key, overwrite on change).

- Query `type`: `preview` (default, compact) or `image` (full resolution) — independent per request.
- Within TTL: serve our stored file **without** hitting WhatsApp.
- After TTL (or `refresh=true`): revalidate via IQ; compare `id`/sha256; download+overwrite only if changed.
- Privacy / no pic: delete stored object (no orphans).
- Binary stream: `GET.../profile-picture/file`.
- Background auto-fetch (own profile + picture push) uses env `AVATAR_FETCH_TYPES` (`both`|`image`|`preview`).
Do not spam `refresh` (WhatsApp rate-overlimit).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Which size to resolve/store for **this** request. `preview` = compact (default, cheap for lists); `image` = full resolution. Own-profile auto-sync and picture notifications follow env `AVATAR_FETCH_TYPES` (default `both`: image first, preview fallback). Instance `avatarUrl` prefers stored full-res when present.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: Force refresh from WhatsApp (default false; cache 24h)

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Phone digits or JID of the contact

picture.description: Avatar payload; `url` points to our durable storage (or API file path), not ephemeral WA CDN

jid.description: Resolved contact JID used for the query

reason.description: When picture is null: not-authorized | item-not-found | privacy | …

cacheTtlSeconds.description: Revalidation TTL (seconds); default 86400 — controls WA IQ, not file lifetime

  -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \

post.summary: Audio blast — call + play predefined audio + record response
post.description:
Outbound VoIP **audio blast**: dial → wait for answer → play WAV → optional remote-leg record + Whisper STT.

### Audio
- **WAV only** (PCM 8/16/24/32-bit or float32). Any rate/channels → resampled to **16 kHz mono**.
- Provide **one** of: `audioUrl` (HTTPS, SSRF-guarded), `mediaBase64`, or multipart field `file` / `audio`.
- Size caps: blast WAV download limit (~20 MiB) and env `MEDIA_UPLOAD_MAX_BYTES` for uploads.

### Recording & STT
- With `recordResponse` (default true) the remote PCM is stored and linked on the call row — `GET .../calls/{callId}/recording` and `POST .../transcribe` work afterwards.
- Transcription runs when `transcribe` is not false **and** `STT_ENABLED` + `STT_API_URL` + `STT_API_KEY` are set (Groq Whisper recommended).

### Timeouts
The HTTP request stays open until the blast finishes. Raise your client timeout for long WAVs (`callTimeoutMs` + audio duration + `responseTimeoutMs`).

**Example**
```bash
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/calls/blast" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"to":"5511999999999","audioUrl":"https://cdn.example.com/prompt.wav","responseTimeoutMs":5000,"recordResponse":true,"transcribe":true,"sttLanguage":"pt"}'
```

get.description:
Unidirectional live event stream (messages, connection, presence, calls, …).

**Auth: put the API key in headers**, not in the URL (avoids access logs, proxies, Referer).

### curl (recommended)
```bash
curl -N -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY" -H "Accept: text/event-stream" \
  "$BASE/v1/events?instance=sales-1"
```

### Browser (fetch + stream — can send headers)
```js
const res = await fetch(`/v1/events?instance=sales-1`, {
  headers: { "X-Api-Key": key, Accept: "text/event-stream" },
})
// read res.body with TextDecoder…
```

Native `EventSource` cannot set headers — only then use `?apiKey=` (discouraged).

- **Instance keys** are always scoped to their instance.
- **Admin** may omit `instance` (all) or filter with `instance=`.
- First event: `{ "event": "connected", "role", "instance", "timestamp" }`.
- Keepalive: SSE comments every 15s (`: ping …`).

**WebSocket upgrade** for bidirectional live VoIP audio (not a plain HTTP JSON response).

### URL
```
ws(s)://<host>/v1/instances/{name}/calls/{callId}/stream?apiKey=<key>
```

Auth: query `apiKey` (browsers) and/or header `X-Api-Key`.

### Protocol
1. Server → JSON text: `{ "op": "ready", "sampleRate": 16000, "channels": 1, "format": "f32le", "callId": "..." }`
2. Client → server **binary**: Float32 LE mono PCM @ 16 kHz (microphone)
3. Server → client **binary**: same format (peer audio)
4. Backpressure JSON: `{ "op": "backpressure", "pause": true | false, "bufferedMs": N }`
5. End: `{ "op": "ended", "callId": "..." }` then socket close

Uses `setExternalAudioMode` — **no file autoplay**. Multi-call supported up to `VOIP_MAX_CONCURRENT_CALLS`.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Liveness and readiness probes (public — no API key).

tags.name: Auth
tags.description:
Authentication uses **two key types**:

1. **Admin** — `ADMIN_API_KEY` from environment. Full access: create/list/delete instances, rotate keys, act on any instance.
2. **Instance** — per-instance `apiKey`, returned on create/rotate **and** on list/get. Scoped to that instance only.

Send the key as:
- Header `X-Api-Key: <key>` **(preferred)**
- or `Authorization: Bearer <key>`

Dashboard login uses the same keys via `GET /v1/me`.

tags.name: Instances
tags.description:
Multi-session lifecycle. Each instance is one WhatsApp linked device (zapo `sessionId`).

List/get include `apiKey`, `pushName`, and `avatarUrl` when known. Instance keys may `GET /v1/instances/{name}` for their own session.

**Typical flow:**
1. `POST /v1/instances` (admin) → receive `apiKey`
2. `POST /v1/instances/{name}/connect`
3. `GET /v1/instances/{name}/qr` → scan with WhatsApp → Linked devices
4. Status becomes `open` — send messages / receive webhooks; poll get for meJid/avatar

Alternatively use pairing code: `POST.../pairing-code` after connect when status is `pairing`/`qr`.

tags.name: Messages
tags.description:
Send WhatsApp messages through a connected instance (`status: open`).

Supports **text**, **image**, **audio** (incl. PTT), and **document**. Video is not exposed in this API.

`to` accepts digits with country code or full JID. Media: provide `mediaUrl` (HTTPS) or `mediaBase64`.

tags.name: Profile
tags.description:
Own WhatsApp profile for a session: get snapshot, set **push name**, set/delete **avatar**, set about status.

- `PUT /v1/profile/name` — display name (`name` or `pushName`, max 25)
- `PUT /v1/profile/image` — JPEG via `mediaUrl` or `mediaBase64` (alias: `/profile/picture`)
- `DELETE /v1/profile/image` — remove avatar

Named paths under `/v1/instances/:name/profile/...` work for admin; short form needs an instance key.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description:
Contact utilities: check if numbers exist on WhatsApp (LID sync) and fetch profile pictures.

Instance must be connected. Rate limits may apply on WhatsApp side for bulk checks.

tags.name: Presence
tags.description: Presence and chat-state (typing / recording). Chat-state is per conversation; presence is account-level.

tags.name: Calls
tags.description:
**Live VoIP audio only** (WhatsApp voice calls via `@zapo-js/voip`).

1. `POST.../calls` with `{ "to": "5511…" }` → `{ callId }`
2. Open WebSocket: `GET /v1/instances/{name}/calls/{callId}/stream?apiKey=...`
3. Exchange binary frames: **Float32 LE mono PCM @ 16 kHz**
4. Control: accept / reject / end / mute

No file autoplay when the peer answers — real phone-style streaming.
Multiple concurrent calls per instance up to `VOIP_MAX_CONCURRENT_CALLS`.

tags.name: Metrics
tags.description: Per-instance analytics: message/call time series for charts, media storage breakdown, and process resource snapshots (memory/CPU are process-wide; storage is per-instance).

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description:
When `webhookUrl` is set on an instance, events are **HTTP POST** JSON:

```json
{
 "event": "message.inbound",
 "instance": "sales-1",
 "timestamp": "2026-07-11T12:10:00.000Z",
 "data": { }
}
```

**Events:** `instance.qr`, `instance.connection`, `instance.paired`, `instance.logged_out`, `message.inbound`, `call.incoming`, `call.state`, `call.ended`.

Filter with `webhookEvents` on create (empty = all). Delivery is best-effort with timeout `WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS`.
